chapter_title.en: Chapter 31 – Satisfaction with the Judgement (of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/ 8)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/ 8)
text: رَأْسُ طَاعَةِ اللَّهِ الصَّبْرُ وَ الرِّضَا عَنِ اللَّهِ فِيمَا أَحَبَّ الْعَبْدُ أَوْ كَرِهَ وَ لَا يَرْضَى عَبْدٌ عَنِ اللَّهِ فِيمَا أَحَبَّ أَوْ كَرِهَ إِلَّا كَانَ خَيْراً لَهُ فِيمَا أَحَبَّ أَوْ كَرِهَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu UUmeyr, from Jameel Bin Salih, from one of the Sheykhs of the clan of Al Najjashy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The chief of obedience to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> is the patience and the satisfaction from Allah<sup>azwj</sup> regarding whatever the servant loves or abhors; and a servant would not be satisfied from Allah<sup>azwj</sup> regarding whatever he loves or abhors except it was better for him regarding what He<sup>azwj</sup> Loves or Abhors’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Jamil ibn Salih from certain shaykhs of Banu al-Najashi from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The head of obedience to Allah is patience and agreeing with the decisions of Allah in all matters whether a servant may love or dislike doing so. Of the servant of Allah anyone who agrees with the decisions of Allah in all matters which he loves or dislikes, should know that agreeing, in fact, is in his best interest.”
